description: A Solar Energy Systems Engineer is designing a 50 MW PV plant with a 20 MW / 80 MWh battery in Phoenix, AZ. Because the PV array is oversized (DC/AC ratio 1.25), the inverter experiences clipping; a DC-coupled battery can capture this clipped energy, while an AC-coupled battery cannot. Using the PVWatts Battery performance model and Single Owner financial model, compare AC-connected and DC-connected battery scenarios. Configure the system with 50,000 kW DC capacity, a 1.25 DC-to-AC ratio, 1-axis tracking, and a 20,000 kW / 80,000 kWh Lithium-ion battery using a bundled TMY weather file for Phoenix,
